title: FIFA World Cup winners list: Know the champions - Olympics.com
content: Brazil have won the FIFA World Cup five times, the most in history, while Germany and Italy have won it four times each. FIFA World Cup 2022 winners Argentina. Argentina are the reigning world champions, having won the **FIFA World Cup 2022** in Qatar. It was also the first FIFA World Cup to be held in the middle of the club football season. Uruguay also went on to lift the trophy at home, beating Argentina 4-2 in the final to be crowned the first FIFA World Cup champions. Uruguay won their second title in 1950 and West Germany lifted their first FIFA World Cup trophy in 1954. Brazil are the most successful team at the FIFA World Cup, having won the title five times. They are also the only country to play in all 22 editions of the FIFA World Cup. Brazil’s first title came in 1958 as the 17-year-old Pele, who would go on to become a legend, scored six goals in the tournament.

title: Brazil remains the most successful... - The Educated Monkey
content: Brazil remains the most successful men’s FIFA World Cup nation with five titles, followed by Germany and Italy with four each. Argentina has won three titles, while France and Uruguay have two each. Italy became the first nation to win back-to-back World Cups in 1934 and 1938, while Brazil later repeated the achievement in 1958 and 1962. Brazil’s famous 1970 team became the first to win three titles, and the country extended its record to five after defeating Germany 2–0 in the 2002 final. Since 1982, the tournament era has been more balanced, with Argentina, France, Germany, Italy, Spain, and Brazil all winning championships. Italy lifted the trophy in 1982 and 2006, Argentina in 1986 and 2022, Germany in 1990 and 2014, France in 1998 and 2018, while Spain won its first title in 2010. In fact, it was the first time they EVERY played each other at a WorldCup. And then it all fell apart and only Germany made it to the final since.
